name: CCTV Operator
description: An NVIDIA-only CCTV co-worker. Give it a local input folder of recordings or one approved RTSP/RTMP stream, visual targets, alert policy, and output folder; it prepares sampled frames with CUDA-enabled FFmpeg and writes source-grounded JSON and Markdown review reports.

alert_policy.description: Cooldown, confidence, and notification policy for reviewer notices.

attention_instruction.description: Operator instruction for a detail to watch in the current stream.
attention_instruction.example: Pay attention to the red backpack near the left doorway.

slack_destination.description: Optional approved Slack destination for alert delivery.

source_mode.description: Select folder to process staged local recordings or stream to monitor one RTSP/RTMP source.

video_source.description: Approved RTSP/RTMP URI used only when source_mode is stream.

visual_targets.description: Configured subjects, objects, or activities to detect.

input_folder.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

output_folder.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

contract.summary: Analyze a staged folder of recordings or one approved RTSP/RTMP stream on a qualifying NVIDIA node, with CUDA-accelerated frame preparation and durable review reports.
users: Video operators, security reviewers, safety operations teams, and site-access coordinators.

steps.goal: Validate the selected recording folder or RTSP/RTMP stream and start the analysis session.
steps.label: Start Video Monitor

steps.goal: Emit frame ticks according to the configured sample interval.
steps.label: Sample Video Frames

steps.goal: Analyze sampled frames for configured targets and alert status.
steps.label: Detect Visual Targets

steps.goal: Merge the latest observation into cumulative JSON and Markdown review reports.
steps.label: Write CCTV Report

metadata.agent_role: AI assistant for reviewing recorded or live video activity and preparing visual detection reports and alerts.
metadata.customizable_for: Video monitoring, configurable visual detection, facility operations, and site safety workflows.
metadata.description: An NVIDIA-only CCTV co-worker. Give it a local input folder of recordings or one approved RTSP/RTMP stream, visual targets, alert policy, and output folder; it prepares sampled frames with CUDA-enabled FFmpeg and writes source-grounded JSON and Markdown review reports.

human_control.reason: The workflow surfaces notable visual changes to reviewers but does not execute physical security actions by itself.

fields.description: Choose folder for staged recordings or stream for one RTSP/RTMP source.
fields.label: Source mode
Input folder
fields.description: Folder whose supported video files are staged and analyzed in folder mode.
Approved RTSP/RTMP URI used only in stream mode.
fields.label: Stream URI

fields.description: Seconds between sampled frames. Twenty seconds avoids inference backlog on one DGX Spark while remaining configurable.
fields.label: Frame sample interval
Visual targets
fields.description: Subjects, objects, or activities to detect in recordings or the approved stream.

fields.label: Alert policy
fields.description: Confidence, cooldown, and notification policy for reviewer notices.

fields.label: Output folder
fields.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

init_config_review.instruction: When running in OtterDesk, show these overwriteable fields during init config and ask the user to change them or keep the defaults before launch.

input_contract.description: Select folder mode to analyze every supported staged recording, or stream mode to sample one approved RTSP/RTMP source on a qualifying NVIDIA node.

optional_inputs.description: Operator instruction for a detail to watch in the current stream.
optional_inputs.example: Pay attention to the red backpack near the left doorway.
optional_inputs.name: attention_instruction

optional_inputs.description: Optional approved Slack destination for alert delivery.
optional_inputs.name: slack_destination
input_folder
optional_inputs.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

optional_inputs.name: output_folder
optional_inputs.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

connector.description: Approved camera stream metadata supplied by a site video connector.

file.description: JSON object loaded from a local file path.

json.description: Inline JSON object supplied through config or runtime overrides.

live_stream.description: Runtime configuration for one approved RTSP/RTMP source.

mock.description: Bundled sample folder with deterministic quick-test detection behavior.

recording_folder.description: A local folder staged into the selected NVIDIA runtime node.

required_inputs.description: Select folder or stream source behavior.
required_inputs.name: source_mode

required_inputs.description: Configured subjects, objects, or activities to detect.
required_inputs.name: visual_targets

required_inputs.description: Cooldown, confidence, and notification policy for reviewer notices.
required_inputs.name: alert_policy

artifacts.description: Stable run-store files and domain-specific outputs.

human.description: Human notices, approval requests, responses, timeouts, and applied decisions.

input_skills.description: Connector-backed or reusable skill-backed input sources.

inputs.description: Resolved runtime inputs and adapter provenance.

logs.description: Runtime stdout/stderr and explicit log artifacts.

output_skills.description: Optional fan-out destinations after local run-store writes.

outputs.description: Primary result and final user-facing artifact.

resources.description: Resource samples such as token, runtime, connector, and disk usage.

streams.description: Named data or event streams exposed by the blueprint.

knowledge_rag.description: Optional local blueprint knowledge retrieval used to ground actor prompts and tool planning.

agentic_research.description: Bounded LLM-selected or actor-guided tool contract for domain work.

metadata.llm_agent_role: AI assistant for reviewing recorded or live video activity and preparing visual detection reports and alerts.
metadata.name: CCTV Operator
metadata.output: Visual detection observations with count, label, category, color, position, activity, confidence, and alert status.

final_artifact.description: The durable user-facing artifact should summarize the result, recommendation, confidence, evidence, next steps, and source references.

output_skills.activation: Set MN_BLUEPRINT_EVENTS_WS_URL and enable this output skill via config override to stream events.
output_skills.name: websocket_events

output_contract.stdout: single JSON object, event envelope, or bundle path

metadata.problem_solved: One NVIDIA-only workflow now handles both staged recording folders and approved RTSP/RTMP streams, preserving source provenance in cumulative review reports.

quick_test.description: Quick test mode shortens the sample interval, disables Slack, and uses mock visual detection unless OLLAMA_LIVE_IN_QUICK_TEST=1.

runtime_features: actor-style LLM specialists use retrieved policy context when available
metadata.simulation_type: Recording-folder or live-stream sampling with visual detection review state.

standard.execution_model: load_metadata -> resolve_config -> load_inputs -> start_run_store -> observe_simulation_state -> call_llm_agent -> evaluate_workflow_state -> route_by_context -> request_human_input -> apply_decision_to_simulation -> emit_events -> write_final_artifact -> fan_out_output_skills

metadata.target_user: Video operators, security reviewers, safety operations teams, and site-access coordinators.
metadata.target_users: Video operators, security reviewers, safety operations teams, and site-access coordinators.

video_source.description: Folder mode processes all supported staged recordings; stream mode samples one approved RTSP/RTMP URI. CUDA-enabled FFmpeg is the only media-preparation path.

arguments.browser_publish_source: Disabled; the shared dashboard observes run-store artifacts and does not republish camera media.
arguments.browser_video_source: Optional preview source configured independently from the NVIDIA analysis source.
arguments.run_id: MirrorNeuron run id used to resolve ~/.mn/runs/<run_id>.
arguments.url: Shared support Gradio app URL written to web_ui.json.

web_ui.description: Shared blueprint support Gradio dashboard rendered from the blueprint's Grafana JSON model and live run-store events.

knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve approved CCTV policy for target detections, source provenance, cooldown behavior, and review-only alert boundaries.
knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved local blueprint knowledge to improve actor review prompts; do not invent facts when retrieval is unavailable.
knowledge_rag.privacy: index local blueprint knowledge only; do not index private run documents unless a future runtime explicitly opts in

agentic_research.note: CCTV actors ground detections in staged files or the configured stream, frame evidence, and alert policy.

knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve approved CCTV policy for target detections, source provenance, cooldown behavior, and review-only alert boundaries.
knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved local blueprint knowledge to improve actor review prompts; do not invent facts when retrieval is unavailable.
knowledge_rag.privacy: index local blueprint knowledge only; do not index private run documents unless a future runtime explicitly opts in

agentic_research.note: CCTV actors ground detections in staged files or the configured stream, frame evidence, and alert policy.
